title: Add OnBuild support for Dockerfiles
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Replaces https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/686
@TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind PTAL
bot, retest this please
Fixes https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/658

@rhatdan I'll play more with this today, but it looks like you've the same issue I had.  But I think the config additions that you put in gave me a thought.

Here's the last bit of test results with your changes:

```
]# buildah bud -f ~/Dockerfile-2 -t result-image .
STEP 1: FROM onbuild-image
STEP 2: RUN touch /baz
STEP 3: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.override_kernel_check=true]localhost/result-image:latest
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:891e1e4ef82ad02a4ea1f030831f942d722c7694c4db64ca3239c8163b811c58
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:4487d6d64f2494619f176ed275ad8fee4b737ef1017e1f43873a5cf1027e4052
Copying blob sha256:d3a7b3612d6d70d927c9664bb56e9d7881226e9d579309172ea772f1efedefca
 98 B / 98 B [==============================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:18d727f1affda9561f1471e5261e08a9212ea8eadf34d9f2fd3955cb084f3816
 1.75 KiB / 1.75 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
18d727f1affda9561f1471e5261e08a9212ea8eadf34d9f2fd3955cb084f3816
[root@localhost buildah]# buildah from result-image
result-image-working-container
[root@localhost buildah]# buildah run result-image-working-container ls /bar
ls: cannot access '/bar': No such file or directory
```
It touched /baz not /bar.

@rhatdan, yep, the first Dockerfile touched 'foo', the second touched 'baz'.  However the OnBuild command in the first Dockerfile touched "bar' and that's what's missing in the resulting container.  I did the bar/baz dance a few times myself while testing.

```
# cat ~/Dockerfile
FROM fedora:28
RUN touch /foo
ONBUILD RUN touch /bar

# cat ~/Dockerfile-2
FROM onbuild-image
RUN touch /baz

# buildah run result-image-working-container ls /baz
/baz

# buildah run result-image-working-container ls /foo
/foo

# buildah run result-image-working-container ls /bar
ls: cannot access '/bar': No such file or directory

```

I played some more today.  I adjusted cmd/buildah/config.go to have it accept OnBuild in my sandbox.  I did it really cheesey, just doing this at the end of updateConfig():
```
        //TOM: delete next 2 lines
        tomString := []string {"RUN touch bar"}
        builder.SetOnBuild(tomString)
```
When I ran `buildah config` and then did `buildah inspect` of the newly created container, I saw "RUN touch bar" value in both OnBuild fields in the json.  I then did `buildah commit` and when I inspected the image both OnBuild fields were empty.  I think something might be going on in the commit code that's now quite right.  That's my next exploration.

This next part might be a red herring.  In the NewImageSource() function in the image.go file, I verified that we're dealing with a v1.MediaTypeImageManifest which stores aside the values from the oconfig.  The oci config does not have an OnBuild variable associated with it that I can find.  Should it?

Looking at the other possible manifest type there, docker.V25MediaTypeManifest, there is an OnBuild variable in there which I can add too, but that whole manifest is ignored in this scenario.

More digging tomorrow, thought I throw this out in case it uncovered anything.

Well OnBuild only works with doing --format docker.  Since OCI Does not understand OnBuild.  We are currently clearing the images OnBuild fields before adding new ones, which might be why it is dropping your config.  

BTW I will bin in Westford on Tuesday.  Maybe we can finish this up then.

@TomSweeneyRedHat This is now passing tests including the one for OnBuild and now you can add configuration via the buildah config --onbuild command.
PTAL

Testing looks good.  I want to play just a bit more:

```
# buildah bud -f ~/Dockerfile --format=docker -t onbuild-image .
STEP 1: FROM fedora:28
STEP 2: RUN touch /foo
STEP 3: ONBUILD RUN touch /bar
STEP 4: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.override_kernel_check=true]localhost/onbuild-image:latest
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:891e1e4ef82ad02a4ea1f030831f942d722c7694c4db64ca3239c8163b811c58
Copying blob sha256:dcce965cf0594c67e904f2837227e12f8276cc899231688a58f2451a01fb9da6
 141 B / 141 B [============================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:009376b6ab528c2d4c8408ff2ee97aaead7c23d4e5bd1fa470fed7d4b601d388
 2.40 KiB / 2.40 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

# buildah bud -f ~/Dockerfile-2 --format=docker -t result-image .
STEP 1: FROM onbuild-image
STEP 2: RUN touch /bar
STEP 3: RUN touch /baz
STEP 4: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.override_kernel_check=true]localhost/result-image:latest
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:891e1e4ef82ad02a4ea1f030831f942d722c7694c4db64ca3239c8163b811c58
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:7abab12163e18b8b2918d3512fdeb52db86560d22eb146338dc3ef78ef46daf8
Copying blob sha256:5a41e19ec68e0213b95659d69ed94eff9fd30e718e6a1a7e49e472e66167196a

Copying config sha256:bab16466828eda3ea8e28a80cf1f74126a818da6c9d9235b71319ea882036799
 2.60 KiB / 2.60 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
bab16466828eda3ea8e28a80cf1f74126a818da6c9d9235b71319ea882036799

# ctr=$(buildah from result-image)

# buildah run $ctr ls /bar
/bar
```
@TomSweeneyRedHat I added a test to make sure `buildah config --onbuild ` works.

@rhatdan I tested again and this time with commit and everything seemed to still work with those lines that I noted commented out.  I'm not seeing any effect of them running, I believe they're all no-ops.

Also in my review @nalind noted a concern that `buildah from` might not being handling "ONBUILD" correctly, and his concern seems founded.  The OnBuild command doesn't seem to be getting fired via that command, but if I build a Dockerfile with only "FROM onbuildimage" in it and then call `buildah bud` then the ONBUILD command gets fired.
@TomSweeneyRedHat If I remove ClearOnBuild, I see the second container image that I base on the first container image include the first images onbuild lines.

Red Hat CI has an unusual error in from.go.  Is that what you're working on?  The code LGTM, needs a cut and then paste in the test and s/b gtg.

Error:
```
can't load package: package github.com/projectatomic/buildah/vendor/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/otremote: 
vendor/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/otremote/remoteadd.go:1:1: expected 'package', found 'EOF'
cmd/buildah/from.go:71::warning: cyclomatic complexity 41 of function fromCmd() is high (> 40) (gocyclo)
make: *** [Makefile:51: validate] Error 1
make: Leaving directory '/root/gopath/src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah'
```
bot, retest this please.

@umohnani8 @TomSweeneyRedHat @mheon @ipbabble PTAL
Finally passes tests.
@giuseppe @mheon @umohnani8 PTAL
LGTM minus wording on one error message
